title: High-Velocity Multi-Merchant Card Exploitation with Clean Individual Transactions
narrative: Compromised card credentials are systematically tested across numerous distinct merchants within a compressed timeframe, generating elevated card-counter aggregations while each individual transaction appears benign. The fraudster orchestrates purchases at 8-15+ different merchant IDs within a rolling window, exploiting the card's validity across diverse retail categories. Per-transaction amounts remain within the cardholder's historical spending range (typically $80-$170), and individual merchant velocity appears normal. However, C8 (card-merchant diversity counter) spikes to p90+ levels, revealing the card is being broadcast across an abnormally wide merchant footprint. C11 and C13 counters similarly elevate as the card touches multiple product categories and address combinations never seen in legitimate cardholder behavior. Device fingerprints may rotate across transactions, but each device-card pairing looks plausible in isolation, masking the coordinated nature of the attack.
indicators: C8 >= 29 (p90+), indicating card used across 29+ distinct merchant contexts in rolling window
C11 >= 27 (p90+), card-address diversity counter showing abnormally high unique addr1 combinations
C1 >= 60 (p90+), elevated total card transaction counter in recent window
C13 between 20-30 (p75-p90 range), card touching many distinct product categories
TransactionAmt between 80-180, within typical cardholder range masking aggregate abuse
D1 rotating across multiple values in card's recent history, indicating device-switching behavior
card1_distinct_addr1 > 8, card associated with abnormally many shipping addresses in window
indicator_explanations: Card has been used at an unusually high number of different merchants recently
Card is associated with far more unique billing addresses than normal cardholder patterns
Card shows elevated total transaction activity in the recent rolling time window
Card is being used across many different product types inconsistent with prior behavior
Individual transaction amount fits cardholder's typical spending range concealing the pattern
Multiple different devices have been paired with this card in rapid succession
Card linked to excessive distinct shipping addresses suggesting systematic credential testing
summary: Stolen card credentials are exploited across 25-30+ distinct merchants in a short window, with each transaction appearing normal in amount and individual velocity, but card-level counters reveal systematic multi-merchant abuse inconsistent with any legitimate shopping pattern.
reasoning: No legitimate cardholder uses their card across 29+ different merchants in a compressed timeframe while maintaining normal per-transaction amounts. The elevated C8, C11, and C1 counters combined with device rotation reveal coordinated credential exploitation where fraudsters test card validity across maximum merchant diversity before the compromise is detected.
